title: Timezone-Displaced Night Transactions Following Credential Compromise
narrative: Cardholder historically transacts 9am-11pm EST with consistent addr1 region, then card experiences burst of activity 2am-5am EST (matching 2pm-5pm Malaysia/Singapore business hours). Fraudster tests card with $1.47 digital goods purchase at 2:14am, then escalates to $847 electronics at 2:31am and $1,235 at 3:08am, all ProductCD=W with new P_emaildomain suffix .my or .sg. Legitimate card never showed is_night=1 transactions in prior 180 days. The txn_hour distribution shifts dramatically: 90-day history shows zero transactions in hours 1-5, then suddenly 4 transactions cluster in this window within 3 hours. Card1_txn_count_24h jumps from baseline 0.8 to 4, while dist1 (address distance) exceeds 8000 miles. AVS mismatch on all night transactions despite previous 98% AVS match rate.
indicators: Zero transactions in txn_hour range 1-5 over 90-day history, then 4+ transactions in 3-hour night window
P_emaildomain switches to .my, .sg, or .ph suffix during is_night=1 transactions
dist1 exceeds 8000 miles from historical addr1 centroid during night burst
card1_txn_count_3h increases from baseline 0.1 to 3+ during hours 2-5am cardholder timezone
AVS match rate drops from 95%+ historical to 0% during night window
TransactionAmt escalates 500x+ from initial test ($1-3) to $800+ within 90 minutes
ProductCD remains W (digital/electronics) across all night transactions with no prior W purchases
summary: A card that never transacted between 1-5am suddenly shows 4+ purchases in a 3-hour night window, with email domains switching to .my/.sg suffixes and address distances exceeding 8000 miles from the cardholder's normal location. Transaction amounts escalate from $1-3 test charges to $800+ purchases within 90 minutes, all showing AVS mismatches despite a historical 95%+ match rate.
reasoning: A fraudster in Southeast Asia (Malaysia/Singapore timezone) has stolen the card credentials and is operating during their daytime business hours, which corresponds to the middle of the night for the legitimate cardholder. The initial small test transaction confirms the card is active before quickly escalating to high-value electronics purchases, behavior impossible for a sleeping cardholder in their home timezone.
indicator_explanations: Cardholder never shops at night, then suddenly four purchases in middle of night
Email domain flips to Southeast Asian provider during overnight transaction spree
Transactions originate from opposite side of the planet during nighttime hours
Card activity spikes from dormant to three purchases in early morning darkness
Address verification suddenly fails completely after months of consistent matches
Attacker tests with tiny amounts then rapidly escalates to maximum theft values
Card exclusively buys electronics overnight despite zero history of such purchases
